record_identity.title: Execution-boundary arbitration uncertainty following proportional authority classification

summary: Following implementation of proportional tool invocation authority bands in CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02, observations suggest some agentic or tool-mediated workflows may exhibit increased hesitation, ambiguity, degraded execution confidence, or difficulty progressing between contextual retrieval, execution-intent assessment, and execution authorisation. The observation does not establish causation between the governance-text amendment and any platform outage or degradation event, but records a potentially relevant post-implementation behavioural pattern for continued monitoring.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This observation matters because preventing premature tool invocation solves only part of the execution-governance problem. Systems may also require positive arbitration pathways that define when sufficient authority exists to proceed. Without such pathways, assistants may become overly cautious, hesitant, or operationally degraded despite remaining compliant with execution-boundary constraints. The observation suggests a potential governance gap between execution restraint and execution authorisation.

source_records.source_context: Review initiated after observing potential uncertainty regarding execution-boundary transitions, authority classification, and execution confidence following adoption of proportional tool invocation authority bands.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary observation linking governance repair activity to subsequent execution-boundary arbitration questions.

cam_internal.governance_layer: runtime governance / execution-boundary arbitration / authority classification / execution authorisation
routing_note: This observation does not assert that PATCH-0002 caused platform degradation or outages.
This observation records a potentially important governance question emerging after implementation of PATCH-0002.
The observation concerns uncertainty regarding transitions between contextual retrieval, execution-intent determination, and execution authorisation.
Future governance work may require arbitration-specific guidance governing authority acquisition and execution confidence.
CAM instruments remain internal governance references and are therefore recorded under cam_internal rather than linked_records.standards.

observation_status.observed_pattern: Potential increase in execution-boundary uncertainty following introduction of proportional authority classification.
observation_status.repair_linkage: Observation emerged after implementation of VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002 and may inform future arbitration-layer development.
observation_status.monitoring_note: Monitor for recurring patterns where systems successfully avoid premature execution but struggle to determine when sufficient authority exists to proceed.

next_action: Continue monitoring. Compare future observations against OBS-0004, FM-0001, and PATCH-0002. Evaluate whether a dedicated arbitration-governance instrument or Annex D amendment is required.
review_observation: Current evidence supports a governance-design question rather than a confirmed failure mode. Additional observations are required before determining whether a distinct failure taxonomy entry or repair pathway should be established.
migration_notes: Created as a post-patch observation rather than a failure mode.
Preserves distinction between temporal correlation and demonstrated causation.
Links execution-boundary uncertainty to prior tool-invocation governance work.
Prepared for possible future Annex D arbitration review.
Intended to remain in watching state pending additional evidence.
